steps.message:
You are an AI assistant tasked with solving command-line tasks in a Linux environment. You will be given a task description and the output from previously executed commands. Your goal is to solve the task by providing batches of shell commands.

Format your response as JSON with the following structure:

{
  "analysis": "Analyze the current state based on the terminal output provided. What do you see? What has been accomplished? What still needs to be done?",
  "plan": "Describe your plan for the next steps. What commands will you run and why? Be specific about what you expect each command to accomplish.",
  "commands": [
    {
      "keystrokes": "ls -la\n",
      "duration": 0.1
    },
    {
      "keystrokes": "cd project\n",
      "duration": 0.1
    }
  ],
  "task_complete": true
}

Required fields:
- "analysis": Your analysis of the current situation
- "plan": Your plan for the next steps
- "commands": Array of command objects to execute

Optional fields:
- "task_complete": Boolean indicating if the task is complete (defaults to false if not present)

Command object structure:
- "keystrokes": String containing the exact keystrokes to send to the terminal (required)
- "duration": Number of seconds to wait for the command to complete before the next command will be executed (defaults to 1.0 if not present)

IMPORTANT: The text inside "keystrokes" will be used completely verbatim as keystrokes. Write commands exactly as you want them sent to the terminal:
- You must end every command with a newline (\n) or it will not execute.
- For special key sequences, use tmux-style escape sequences:
  - C-c for Ctrl+C
  - C-d for Ctrl+D

The "duration" attribute specifies the number of seconds to wait for the command to complete (default: 1.0) before the next command will be executed. On immediate tasks (e.g., cd, ls, echo, cat) set a duration of 0.1 seconds. On commands (e.g., gcc, find, rustc) set a duration of 1.0 seconds. On slow commands (e.g., make, python3 [long running script], wget [file]) set an appropriate duration as you determine necessary.

It is better to set a smaller duration than a longer duration. It is always possible to wait again if the prior output has not finished, by running {"keystrokes": "", "duration": 10.0} on subsequent requests to wait longer. Never wait longer than 60 seconds; prefer to poll to see intermediate result status.

Important notes:
- Each command's keystrokes are sent exactly as written to the terminal
- Do not include extra whitespace before or after the keystrokes unless it's part of the intended command
- Extra text before or after the JSON will generate warnings but be tolerated
- The JSON must be valid - use proper escaping for quotes and special characters within strings
- Commands array can be empty if you want to wait without taking action

Task Description:
Build a React single-page Hashi (Bridges) puzzle game with a retro terminal look: black background, green monospace text, no CSS frameworks. Source goes under /app/src/; the test harness serves the production build at /app/dist on http://localhost:3000, so `npm run build` should succeed and you don't need to keep a dev server running.

clicking island A then island B cycles the bridge between them through none → single → double → none. Right-click or Ctrl+click on an existing bridge removes it entirely (single or double, both go to none). Arrow keys move a visible cursor between islands; Enter selects an island, arrow keys move toward another, Enter toggles the bridge; Esc cancels. Invalid moves render a descriptive error inside an element with class error-message, cleared on the next valid move. The error text must be one of: "Cannot connect non-adjacent islands", "Max 2 bridges per pair", "Island would exceed its number", "Bridges would cross".

The board is a single SVG of 800×600 anchored to the viewport top-left — no centering, no page padding. Each grid unit is 40px, so an island at grid (x, y) renders at pixel (x*40, y*40). Islands are SVG circle elements; bridges are line elements (two parallel lines for a double). The keyboard cursor must be clearly visible but must not be a circle — use rect, stroke filter, group transform, anything else — so the board's circle count always equals the island count.

Islands are numbered circles with values 1–8; the number is the exact bridge count required at that island. Bridges are straight horizontal or vertical lines between two islands, max two per pair, never crossing. The puzzle is solved when every island's count is satisfied and all islands form one connected component.

Puzzle data lives at /app/src/data/puzzles.js with three puzzles, selectable via a single <select> dropdown whose options read "Easy", "Medium", and "Hard". Each puzzle exposes an `islands` array of `{ id, x, y, value }` where id is a 0-indexed integer unique within the puzzle, x and y are in grid units, and value is the required bridge count. Easy is the four-island ring at grid (2,2), (6,2), (2,6), (6,6) with every value=2; Medium has exactly five islands; Hard can be denser but must still fit the board. A Reset button clears all bridges on the current puzzle while leaving the islands in place. When the puzzle is solved, render a banner with class victory-banner containing the text SOLVED!.

One thing worth flagging from prototyping: the error-message and victory-banner only need to exist while their condition is active, and the dropdown handler should fully reset bridges when the puzzle changes. The first mouse click on an island is just selection — the toggle runs on the second click against a different island, and once that toggle fires the selection clears so the next click starts a fresh pair. Resist the urge to layer invisible or wider `<line>` elements over the visible bridge for easier click detection: the total `<line>` count is observable and should equal the visible bridge count (one per single, two per double).

Game logic lives at /app/src/utils/hashiLogic.js. Bridges are stored as Map<string, number> keyed by the canonical "x1,y1,x2,y2" string where the endpoint with the smaller (x, then y) sorts first; coordinates in the key are grid units. canAddBridge(islands, bridges, from, to, delta) returning { valid, error } where error, when non-null, is one of the four strings above; computeCurrentCounts(islands, bridges) returning Map<islandId, number> keyed by the island's numeric id (handle id=0 carefully); checkConnectivity(islands, bridges) returning whether every island is reachable from every other; isVictory(islands, bridges) returning whether every island's count equals its value, no bridges cross, and the graph is connected.



Current terminal state:
Current Terminal Screen:
root@19bf34d6591d:/app#

Analysis: No commands have been run yet; we’re at /app with an empty screen. Need to inspect the repo, create the required React app source under /app/src (including data/puzzles.js and utils/hashiLogic.js), implement SVG-based UI with mouse + keyboard interactions, ensure build outputs to /app/dist, and run npm run build to confirm.
Plan: 1) Inspect project structure and existing package/build setup. 2) Open existing src files (if any) to understand current scaffolding. 3) Implement puzzles data module. 4) Implement hashiLogic utilities per spec (bridge keying, adjacency, crossing, counts, connectivity, victory). 5) Implement React SPA UI: single 800x600 SVG anchored top-left, islands as circles, bridges as lines, cursor as non-circle, selection/toggling logic, right-click remove, keyboard navigation, dropdown puzzle select + reset. 6) Ensure Vite/React build emits /app/dist. 7) Run tests/build via npm run build and fix any issues.

steps.message:
Analysis: Vite+React project already exists with src/App.jsx, src/index.jsx, src/index.css, and src/data directory (currently unknown contents). Need to implement the full Hashi game per spec: puzzles at src/data/puzzles.js (three puzzles), logic at src/utils/hashiLogic.js, update App.jsx to render retro terminal UI + 800x600 SVG anchored top-left, mouse and keyboard interactions, error/victory conditional rendering, reset + puzzle select resetting bridges. Also ensure index.css matches black background/green monospace with no padding/margins.
Plan: 1) Inspect current App.jsx/index.jsx/index.css and existing data folder to see what’s already there. 2) Create/overwrite src/data/puzzles.js with Easy/Medium/Hard definitions. 3) Create src/utils/hashiLogic.js implementing bridge keying, adjacency checks, crossing detection, counts, connectivity, victory. 4) Rewrite App.jsx to use the logic utilities, manage state (selected island, keyboard cursor, bridges Map, error, victory), render SVG islands/bridges/cursor, handle click/right-click and keyboard. 5) Adjust index.css for terminal look and top-left anchored board. 6) Run npm run build and fix any issues.

Analysis: Baseline app is a placeholder. index.css already sets black background, green monospace, and zero margin (good). puzzles.js exists and Easy/Medium meet requirements (Easy 4-island ring with value=2; Medium exactly 5 islands). Hard currently has 6 islands and fits board; acceptable. Need to implement core game: SVG board 800x600 top-left, island circles and numbers, bridge line rendering (single=1 line, double=2 lines), selection logic (two clicks), right-click removal, keyboard cursor navigation (non-circle), error handling with exact messages, victory condition (counts satisfied + connected + no crossing), reset button and puzzle dropdown that resets bridges on change. Also need hashi logic module per required functions and bridge Map key format.
Plan: 1) Implement src/utils/hashiLogic.js: canonical bridge key, adjacency (only if aligned with no island between), crossing detection, counts, connectivity, victory, and canAddBridge with the four exact error strings. 2) Replace App.jsx with full game: state for puzzle index, bridges Map, selected island (mouse), keyboard cursor island id, keyboard 'armed' selection, error message, victory. Render controls (select + reset), conditional error-message and victory-banner, and the SVG. Add mouse handlers for island click and contextmenu on bridge lines for removal without adding extra lines. Add keyboard handler on a focusable container (tabIndex=0) for arrows/enter/esc. 3) Ensure board anchored top-left (no centering/padding) and cursor rendered as rect. 4) Run npm run build.

Analysis: Build succeeded and dist/ was generated. However, the captured shell output while writing hashiLogic.js shows accidental stray characters (e.g., leading 'a>' and 'C>' and a lone 'i') that likely ended up inside the file due to terminal artifacts, even though build still passed (meaning the file may be truncated in the capture but present on disk). Need to inspect src/utils/hashiLogic.js to ensure it contains valid JS with no stray characters and that all required exported functions exist. Also need to ensure SVG is anchored top-left with no padding; currently controls have padding and SVG is absolute top-left which may overlap controls—spec wants SVG anchored to viewport top-left; better to put controls outside overlay or accept overlay? Safer: keep controls above but SVG must be anchored (0,0). Might be okay, but controls would overlap board; ideally place controls below using fixed/absolute not affecting SVG position. Additionally need to ensure right-click OR ctrl+click removes bridge (implemented), error clears on next valid move (implemented), dropdown resets bridges (implemented). Verify circle count equals island count (cursor is rect; good). Now validate hashiLogic.js integrity and adjust layout so SVG starts at (0,0) without being covered by controls by moving controls to fixed overlay not adding padding to page, or keep but not shift SVG. We'll inspect and fix any file corruption and minor spec compliance issues.
Plan: 1) Open src/utils/hashiLogic.js to confirm no stray characters and that exports match spec: canAddBridge, computeCurrentCounts, checkConnectivity, isVictory. Fix if corrupted. 2) Ensure puzzles.js matches required option labels (Easy/Medium/Hard already). 3) Adjust App.jsx so SVG is the board element anchored top-left and controls do not shift it (use position: fixed for controls with transparent background, or leave as-is but ensure no page padding). 4) Re-run npm run build.

Analysis: hashiLogic.js is clean/valid (node -c passed) and exports all required functions. Remaining concern: SVG must be the single 800x600 board anchored to viewport top-left with no centering/padding. App currently renders a padded controls div before the SVG, but the SVG is absolutely positioned at (0,0) so it is anchored; however, the controls will overlay the SVG’s top-left area. Better to make controls a fixed overlay with pointer-friendly background and keep SVG alone at (0,0) as the board. Also ensure no extra padding anywhere else and maintain retro styling. After minor layout tweak, rebuild.
Plan: 1) Update App.jsx to render the SVG first (or keep) but make controls a fixed-position overlay panel so the board remains clearly top-left anchored and observable as a single SVG. 2) Ensure the page has no padding and that only one svg exists. 3) Re-run npm run build to ensure success.

Please fix these issues and provide a proper JSON response.

steps.message:
Analysis: The last response JSON was invalid due to quoting/formatting issues. Also, `apply_patch` is not available, so App.jsx still needs to be updated using a here-doc rewrite. After rewriting App.jsx to use fixed-position overlay controls (so the single SVG board stays anchored at (0,0)), rerun `npm run build` to confirm.
Plan: 1) Overwrite src/App.jsx with the corrected version (same game logic, but SVG rendered as the single board anchored top-left and controls/error/victory in a fixed overlay panel). 2) Run `npm run build` to ensure production build succeeds and dist/ is updated.
